name: Graphlit MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server that connects to the Graphlit Platform to provide a searchable, RAG-ready knowledge base. Ingests data from 18+ platforms (Slack, Discord, Google Drive, Jira, GitHub, Outlook, Notion, etc.), auto-extracts documents to Markdown, transcribes audio/video, and exposes search, retrieval, web crawling, structured extraction, and content generation as MCP tools.
use_cases: Unified knowledge base across Slack, GitHub, Jira, and Google Drive
RAG-powered Q&A over ingested documents and messages
Web crawling and search integrated into AI assistant workflows
Structured JSON extraction from unstructured text
not_for: Self-hosted or fully local deployments - requires Graphlit Platform
Free usage beyond trial - requires Graphlit subscription
Simple file search without needing platform integrations
Privacy-sensitive environments that cannot use cloud services
best_when: You need a unified knowledge base spanning many SaaS platforms (Slack, Jira, GitHub, etc.) accessible via MCP, and are willing to use the Graphlit cloud platform.
avoid_when: You need fully self-hosted or local-only RAG, want to avoid vendor lock-in to Graphlit Platform, or have strict data residency requirements.

alternatives.name: haiku-rag
alternatives.note: Local-first RAG with LanceDB, no cloud platform dependency

alternatives.name: Qdrant MCP
alternatives.note: Vector search MCP without the multi-platform ingestion

alternatives.name: Firecrawl
alternatives.note: Web crawling focused, without the knowledge base features

auth.notes: Requires GRAPHLIT_ORGANIZATION_ID, GRAPHLIT_ENVIRONMENT_ID, and GRAPHLIT_JWT_SECRET from Graphlit Platform dashboard. Additional API keys needed for individual connectors (Slack, Discord, GitHub, etc.).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Graphlit Platform has a free tier; limits not specified in MCP server docs
pricing.paid_tiers: Graphlit Platform paid plans (see graphlit.com for details)
pricing.notes: MCP server is MIT licensed open source. Requires Graphlit Platform account which has its own pricing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No error handling documentation in README; relies on platform error responses
known_agent_gotchas: Requires Graphlit Platform signup and three environment variables
Each connector (Slack, GitHub, etc.) needs its own API credentials
Windows users need explicit cmd.exe npx invocation
